title: leucanthemum corsicum
intro: Leucanthemum corsicum, also known as the Pyrenean Oxeye Daisy or Corsican Daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Pyrenees in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. Leucanthemum corsicum has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
